title: Completeness-critic finding
description: Validates the return of a completeness-critic dispatch (SPEC-workflow-phases-2-5 § 4a). FLAG-only: the critic reports gaps; it never authors replacement text and never self-reports blocking status. Blocking is computed deterministically by the orchestrator (affichage + unresolved placeholder OR missing/misplaced disclaimer), never by the agent — so there is no 'blocking' property here (§ 0c invariants #2, #6 and § 4a). additionalProperties:false closes the contract against any field a permissive agent might add.

missing.description: Gaps the critic found in the artifact. Empty when nothing is missing — report an empty array, never omit the property.

category.description: The gap class, as a closed enum so the orchestrator's is_blocking() check is a deterministic membership test (§ 4a). Blocking fires only on affichage + (unresolved_placeholder | missing_disclaimer | misplaced_disclaimer); unaddressed_brief_item and underspecified_section are always advisory. The enum is the single decision input — a free-text category would make blocking undecidable and re-open the silent failure.

field.description: The expected element that is absent or incomplete, e.g. an affichage placeholder token, a required disclaimer name, a narrative section heading.

issue.description: One-line description of why the field is flagged — what is missing, unresolved, or misplaced. Describes the gap only; never proposes the text that would fill it.

location.description: Optional locus of the gap in the artifact (e.g. 'section 3, line 41'). Omit when the gap belongs to no single position. Advisory only — the orchestrator does not gate on it.

coverage_pct.description: Percent of expected elements present in the artifact, 0-100. Advisory signal for the orchestrator's surfacing decision, not a gate value the agent acts on.
